text:
You are a teammate on the swarm-wave team. You have two tasks:

**Task #4: [str-28vd.2] Semgrep CE starter rules** (P2)
Add initial Semgrep CE config for repo-specific architectural constraints. A .semgrep config committed, scripts/quality/check-meta.sh runs Semgrep when installed, starter ruleset includes at least one rule for protocol/frontend parity and one for duplicated config boundaries.

**Task #5: [str-28vd.6] Local hook bootstrap** (P2)
Add repo docs/config for local hook integration. Pre-commit and pre-push call repo-owned quality scripts (scripts/quality/) instead of duplicating command logic.

**Workflow:**
1. Mark task #4 as in_progress with TaskUpdate
2. `bd start str-28vd.2` to create branch
3. Explore existing scripts/quality/ and any .semgrep config
4. Create .semgrep/ directory with starter rules for protocol parity and config boundaries
5. Update scripts/quality/check-meta.sh to run Semgrep when installed
6. Commit, mark task #4 as completed
7. Start task #5: `bd start str-28vd.6` on a new branch
8. Add hook bootstrap docs and/or a setup script (e.g., scripts/setup-hooks.sh)
9. Make pre-commit/pre-push point to scripts/quality/ entrypoints
10. Commit, mark task #5 as completed
11. Send a message to team-lead with results

Note: Separate issues, separate branches.
